_metadata.compiled_by: Researched directly from city/agency CBAs, official pay schedules, and department careers pages.
_metadata.scope: Side-by-side comparison of contract base salary across 13 large U.S. cities for police (5 ranks), firefighter, sanitation, transit, K-12 teacher and 8 other public-sector occupations. All figures sourced from city CBAs, official salary schedules and department careers pages — every cell links to its source.
_metadata.salary_basis: ALL FIGURES ARE BASE SALARY ONLY. Two top-step columns are shown: 'Top base (post-progression)' is the highest base salary an officer reaches by completing the standard step ladder, BEFORE any longevity bonus tiers — this is the apples-to-apples comparison field. 'Max with longevity (rank-and-file)' is the highest base achievable while staying at the same rank, including all longevity steps the contract provides for that rank — provided where published. Both EXCLUDE overtime, holiday pay, uniform allowance, night differential, education incentives (Boston Quinn Bill, etc.), certification pay, court time, detail/specialty pay, and benefits.
_metadata.rank_clarification: All police, fire, sanitation and transit-bus-operator figures are for the LOWEST RANK in that role's classification (rank-and-file patrol officer, rank-and-file firefighter, rank-and-file refuse collector, rank-and-file bus operator). Pay scales for promoted ranks (detective, sergeant, lieutenant, captain; fire engineer, lieutenant, captain, battalion chief; sanitation equipment operator, supervisor; transit dispatcher, supervisor) are SEPARATE schedules and are not included. Most officers spend the bulk of their careers at the lowest rank, so this is the meaningful cross-city comparison.

roles_included.police_officer: Rank-and-file patrol officer, lowest sworn rank, post-academy.
roles_included.firefighter: Rank-and-file firefighter, lowest rank, post-academy. Standard firefighter schedules (commonly 56 hrs/week) are reflected in the published annual figures; these are the contract's annual base.
roles_included.sanitation_worker: City-employed refuse collection truck operator. Several cities contract residential refuse to private firms (Boston, Seattle, San Francisco, in part Phoenix) — those are marked N/A.
roles_included.transit_bus_operator: Bus operator at the regional transit authority that serves the city (MTA, MBTA, SEPTA, WMATA, LA Metro, SFMTA, MTS, King County Metro, CTA, METRO Houston, DART, VIA, Valley Metro). These are typically NOT city employees; they work for the transit authority. San Diego MTS and Phoenix Valley Metro contract operations to private companies.

important_caveats: Civil-service pay schedules are a different number from take-home pay. Most officers/firefighters earn meaningfully more in total compensation due to overtime, longevity, holiday pay, and detail/specialty pay.
'Years to top' refers to the standard step progression in the pay schedule. Some cities have longevity steps that continue past the nominal top step.
Where a city's CBA was unavailable or scanned-image-only, figures may be drawn from official department careers pages, which can lag the current contract by months.
Several CBAs are recently expired or in renegotiation as of compilation date; that is flagged per cell.

rpp_2023._source: U.S. Bureau of Economic Analysis, Regional Price Parities by MSA (MARPP), All Items, 2023.

nyc_all_in_estimates._note: Base salary in NYC contracts is misleadingly low because longevity pay, holiday pay, uniform allowance, night-shift differential and overtime are all paid OUTSIDE the base step. The figures below are reported by the city or department as 'all-in' top-step compensation including those add-ons (excluding overtime). Useful when comparing NYC to cities where longevity is folded into the base step.

police_officer.components: Base $85,292 + longevity (3-yr increments) + holiday pay + night-shift differential + uniform allowance

firefighter.components: Base $105,146 + holiday pay + night differential + 15% night-tour bonus + longevity

police_officer.notes: PBA contract expired 7/31/2025; successor under negotiation. With longevity, holiday pay, uniform allowance and differentials, total comp at top step reaches ~$131,500 (city's 'all-in' figure). Top base step is $85,292 at 5.5 years. NYC keeps longevity OUTSIDE base — paid as separate adders ($350 at 5yr, $1,400 at 10yr, $2,500 at 15yr, $3,600 at 20yr; reflects 2017-25 PBA contract). Plus holiday pay, uniform allowance, night differential. PBA contract EXPIRED 7/31/2025 — successor under negotiation, expected 8-15% retroactive raise.

firefighter.notes: Top base $105,146 is base only; with holiday/night-shift/longevity, top firefighter avg total comp commonly exceeds $130k. $105,146 base reached at 5.5 yrs. FDNY longevity adders paid separately. With holiday pay (5%), night differential (15% of nights), uniform allowance, top firefighter total comp commonly exceeds $130k.

sanitation_worker.notes: CBA term Dec 28, 2022 – Feb 27, 2028. $92,093 base at 5.5 yrs. DSNY longevity adders separate.

transit_bus_operator.notes: Top hourly $41.0875 × 2,080 = $85,462 annualized. CBA term May 2023 – May 2026. Entry rate not in published wage chart — see CBA Appendix.

police_detective.notes: Detective 1st Grade top base under DEA CBA 6/1/2022-5/31/2027 after 6/1/2025 3.50% raise. Det 2nd Grade $134,819; Det 3rd Grade $119,980. 2.25% service differential ($2,721+) at 5 yrs in rank. NB: 'Detective Specialist' titles under PBA contract are different and lower.

police_sergeant.notes: Per April 14, 2025 SBA Unit Agreement: incumbents below top step on 12/10/2024 moved to top step ($134,819). New sergeants follow 6-step schedule (Step 1 $109,651 → Step 6 $134,819). Top step rises to $140,212 effective 12/10/2025.

police_lieutenant.notes: LBA Unit Bargaining Agreement 2022-2027 PDF on OLR is image-only; could not extract base figures. 2017 base was $124,373 top step. Practitioner sources suggest current top base ~$155-175K but not from official source.

police_captain.notes: Most recent posted CEA executed contract on OLR is 2003-2012; current CEA agreement details not located in extractable official PDF. Practitioner sources suggest current top base ~$180-200K (unverified).

dispatcher_911.notes: Police Communications Technician. Top step ~5 yrs; base only, excludes night-shift differential.

police_detective.notes: Detective III, class 2223-3, schedule 8, top step (Step 7) = $181,614. Longevity: +$120/biwk at 10y, +$240 at 15y, +$360 at 20y ($9,396/yr). Max with 20-yr longevity = $191,010 base.

police_sergeant.notes: Sergeant II, class 2227-2, schedule 7, top step = $172,051. Plus $9,396 longevity at 20y = $181,447.

police_lieutenant.notes: Lieutenant II, class 2232-2, top step at schedule 10 = $202,244. With time-in-grade Lt II progresses through schedules 11/12/13 to $237,719.

police_captain.notes: Captain I, class 2244-1, range 9046, Step 7 = $261,522. Captain II $275,887; Captain III $291,225. Per Article 5.2, promotion places employee directly on Step 7.

teacher.notes: LAUSD T-table. Pay Scale Group 1 Level 1 = $66,639. No clean 'BA only top step' — advancement requires units beyond BA. MA top w/ DR differential + 4 Career Increments on C-basis = $116,383.

police_officer.notes: Schedule D, Police Officer Grade D-1, Effective 1/1/2025. Entrance $61,782 → Step 1 (post-academy) $88,170 → Step 6 (4.5yr / 54mo) $111,804 = post-progression base. Then longevity tiers: Step 7 (10yr) $115,686, Step 8 (15yr) $119,976, Step 9 (20yr) $123,444, Step 10 (25yr) $127,758 = max with longevity at rank-and-file.

firefighter.notes: Schedule F, Grade 1, Effective 1/1/2021 (still in 2025 Pay Plan PDF). Step 6 (4.5yr) = $96,528 post-progression base. Longevity tiers: Step 9 (20yr) $108,036; Step 10 (25yr) $111,810 max. NEW 5-yr CBA approved by City Council 10/16/2025 includes 21-25% raise over life with retro pay — figures will rise meaningfully once updated schedule is published.

sanitation_worker.notes: Motor Truck Driver, Schedule P. Top step needs verification — Schedule P shows longevity steps progressing well above $100k at 25 yrs but the Sanitation Laborer / General Laborer ladders are different from Motor Truck Driver. Conservative top-step base not asserted.

police_detective.notes: Phoenix has NO separate Detective civil-service class; detective is an assignment within Officer rank. Same as patrol top-step ($107,827, Class 62210 step 9).

police_captain.notes: Phoenix has NO Captain rank — Commander (Class 62240) is the next rank above Lieutenant. Range $84.30–$105.39/hr; top of range = $219,211. Range-based not stepped (exempt management).

teacher.notes: Phoenix Union HSD. Live PDF page is JS-loaded, can't auto-fetch. District publicly states minimum starting $52,000 / $58,127 with master's; avg salary 'over $70,000' per district announcement. Phoenix is split among many K-8 districts; PUHSD is the largest secondary district.

sanitation_worker.notes: Average DC 33 sanitation worker salary widely reported at ~$42,574 (CBS Philly, July 2025). Title-specific entry/top from city pay ranges database not extracted. Post-strike July 2025 settlement: 3% raises annually for 3 years + new 5th step + $1,500 bonus.

transit_bus_operator.notes: $25.17/hr entry × 2,080; $33.57/hr top × 2,080. Subsequent 3.5%/yr raises in Dec 2025 successor agreement raise these by ~7% over the next two years.

police_sergeant.notes: Q050 Sergeant: $84.84/hr × 2080 = $176,462. Single-step. Eligibility requires min 2 years SFPD service as Q002.

police_captain.notes: Q080 Captain: $122.40/hr × 2080 = $254,592. Single-step, exempt from overtime.

teacher.notes: SFUSD. PSGs by credential and units beyond BA: B6 (BA <30), B7 (BA+30-59), B8 (BA+60+/MA). B6 Step 1 (BA only) $73,689 total; B6 step 21 $109,618 (last step). B8 (BA+60+/MA) substantially higher. Plus QTEA + FWEA add-ons.

police_officer.notes: 1st Year Days base $75,342; 3rd Year base = $92,568 = post-progression base, pre-longevity. Longevity tiers add: 5yr +$4,389 ($96,957), 10yr +$8,776 ($101,344), 15yr +$15,285 ($107,853), 20yr +$17,554 ($110,122), 25yr +$18,250 ($110,818 max). Quinn Bill education incentives add 10-25% on TOP of these (Associates +10%, Bachelors +20%, Masters +25%) — not in figures here.

firefighter.notes: New 4-year CBA 2024-2028 ratified Dec 22, 2025 (8.5% compounded). Salary appendix not yet posted online. New 4-year CBA 2024-2028 ratified Dec 22, 2025 (8.5% compounded). Salary appendix not yet posted on boston.gov.

sanitation_worker.notes: Boston residential refuse collection performed by Capitol Waste Services under contract; current contract expires 2027. No city-employed curbside sanitation workers.

transit_bus_operator.notes: Entry $30/hr × 2,080. Top hourly not in announcement; estimated ~$35-36/hr after 18% cumulative 4-year increase. Entry $30/hr from MBTA announcement; top hourly not in announcement; estimated ~$35-36/hr after 18% cumulative 4-yr increase.

police_captain.notes: $194,000 figure cited by Boston Globe (3/3/2026) for BPD captain base salary; not independently verified from extractable BPSOF salary scale (PDF is scanned image).

police_detective.notes: BPDBS salary scale PDF is scanned image; not text-extractable. Needs OCR pass.

police_sergeant.notes: Salary scale shows top sergeant 3rd-year base around $109-117k depending on tour but rank labels are image-embedded; could not deterministically map figures to rank without OCR.

police_lieutenant.notes: Per Boston Globe 2025 reporting, top-paid Boston cops include lieutenants with base ~$160-180k (unverified).

teacher.notes: BPS, 9-step traditional schedule. Lanes Bachelors, B+15, Masters, M+15..M+75, Doctorate. Figures are BTU 9/1/2023 schedule + 2.5% per 2024-27 contract retro to 9/1/2024. BA Step 1 $66,043; BA Step 9 $110,057; MA Step 9 $116,082.

police_officer.notes: 2024 ratified CBA Step 1 was $103,944; 2025 contract amendment raises entry to ~$118,000 with $126,336 reached at 6 months and $136,104 top base. Step 1 entry $118k under 2025 contract amendment, top step $136,104 at 4.5 yrs. SPOG longevity bonuses paid as separate adders.

firefighter.notes: Recruit $102,048; Step 5 $121,488 at 42 months (hourly $58.41). CBA Dec 2021 – Dec 2026. Recruit $102,048 → Step 5 $121,488 at 42 months. CBA Dec 2021 – Dec 2026.

sanitation_worker.notes: Seattle Public Utilities contracts collection to Recology and Waste Management. No city-employed sanitation workers.

transit_bus_operator.notes: Entry $27.59/hr × 2,080. Top step not extracted from CBA appendix. Contract Nov 2022 – Oct 2025; ~17% cumulative increase suggests top hourly ~$42-45. Entry $27.59/hr; top step not extracted from ATU 587 CBA appendix. ~17% cumulative increase suggests top hourly ~$42-45.

police_detective.notes: Detective in Seattle is an ASSIGNMENT premium (4% of base) on top of Police Officer top step, NOT a separate rank. Top-step PO base $154,248 × 1.04 = $160,418. Detective-Homicide/CSI/FIT = 6% premium ($163,503).

police_sergeant.notes: Police Sergeant top step (18 mos) = $14,790/mo × 12 = $177,480. Patrol premium 1.5% adds for patrol sergeants.

police_lieutenant.notes: Lieutenant top step (30 mos) effective 12/31/2025 = $18,139/mo × 12 = $217,668. Longevity per Appendix A.6: 6% at 15y, 7% at 20y, 12% at 25y, 13% at 30y → max ~$245,965.

police_captain.notes: Captain top step (30 mos) effective 12/31/2025 = $21,571/mo × 12 = $258,852. Precinct Captain assignment +6%; Violent Crimes/Night/Traffic +2-3%.

teacher.notes: SPS Lanes 100 (BA) → 906 (PhD). Lane 100 Step 1 base $61,578 (180-day); Lane 100 caps at Step 12 base $84,947 (TOTAL with contractual/tech/responsibility days $102,025). Lane 906 (PhD) Step 15 base $118,665. Advancement requires both years AND credit hours.

police_officer.notes: FY26 schedule, 4.25% increase Oct 5, 2025. Step 1 $75,433 → Step 9 $111,442. Post-probation Step 1 $78,601; post-5yr retention Step 1 $82,531. FY26 schedule, 4.25% increase Oct 5, 2025. Step 9 of 9 = end of ladder. Post-probation Step 1 $78,601; post-5yr retention Step 1 $82,531 are interim retention bumps.

firefighter.notes: FY25 schedule (3.5% increase as of Oct 8, 2023, posted May 18, 2025). Class 01 Private Step 1 $62,236 → Step 9 $92,113. Through 30-yr longevity Step 9 reaches $110,536. Class 01 Private Step 9 = end of ladder $92,113. Through 30-yr longevity Step 9 reaches $110,536.

sanitation_worker.notes: DC DPW Solid Waste classifications RW-3501-04 (entry) / RW-3501-05 (experienced). Wage Grade RW pay table not extracted from DCHR.

police_detective.notes: Class 03 Detective FY26 schedule (4.25% raise 10/5/2025). Pay #3 Step 7 (top + 5-yr retention) = $138,252. Pay #1 base Step 7 = $126,361. Longevity 30 YOS Pay #8 Step 7 = $166,829.

police_sergeant.notes: Class 04 Sergeant. Pay #1 Step 6 = $130,750 base. Pay #3 Step 6 (5-yr retention top) = $143,054. Longevity 30 YOS Step 6 = $173,745.

police_lieutenant.notes: FY23 schedule (only one located). Class 05 Lieutenant Pay #4 Step 5 (5-yr retention top) = $135,535. Pay #1 base Step 5 = $123,878. FY26 figures should be ~10-12% higher reflecting subsequent COLAs.

police_captain.notes: FY23 schedule. Class 07 Captain Pay #4 Step 4 (5-yr retention top) = $150,904. Pay #1 base Step 4 = $137,925. FY26 figures should be ~10-12% higher.

teacher.notes: DCPS ET-15 10-month schedule. Lanes Bachelors, BA+15, BA+30/Masters, MA+30, MA+60/PhD. Steps 1-11, 12-15 band, then 16. BA top Step 12-15 $102,498 (BA does not get Step 16). MA top Step 16 $126,474. MA+60/PhD top Step 16 $133,623.
